skillName: Solve problems involving tables and timetables
misconceptionSummary: Students misread a timetable and use the wrong row or column, selecting an incorrect service.

lines.text: Need to arrive in Leeds by 10:30
Read the wrong column — used departure not arrival

steps.narration: The student read the time from the wrong row — the departure time at another stop rather than the arrival time at Leeds. Timetable errors almost always come from reading the wrong row or column.

visuals.ruleText: Find the destination row first, then scan left to find a suitable time — then read up to find the departure
visuals.subText: Always confirm which row is arrival and which is departure before reading any time

steps.narration: Fix the destination row first — that anchors you to the correct line. Scan across that row for a time that meets the constraint. Then look up to the departure row in the same column to find when to board.

lines.text: Same column, departure row: 09:05 — board then

steps.narration: In the Leeds row, ten eighteen is before ten thirty — that is the last suitable service. In the same column, the departure row shows nine oh five. Board at nine oh five to arrive by ten thirty.

visuals.label: Find destination row first → suitable time → same column for departure

steps.narration: Board at nine oh five, arrive Leeds at ten eighteen — before the ten thirty deadline. Always anchor to the correct row before reading any time.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: reading the departure time when the question asks about arrival. Find the destination row first, then find the arrival time — then look up in the same column for the departure.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Always identify the arrival row and departure row before reading times. Find the destination row first, pick the time that fits, then read the departure from the same column.
